title: Foundations
summary: Joined How To Hockey as the first official employee. Started building across the stack — frontend, backend, and platform tooling — while learning what it means to own a production system end to end.
highlights: First official hire at How To Hockey, working directly alongside founder Jeremy Rupke
Shipped early platform features across web and mobile
Established initial deployment patterns and release workflows
Built the muscle of designing for users first and engineering second

title: Building Products
summary: Shifted from platform maintenance to product ownership. Designed and shipped multiple full-stack products — membership platforms, mobile apps, and community tools — with full accountability from UX to deployment.
highlights: Designed and launched The Pond, a gated membership and course platform on WordPress
Built TenThousandShotChallenge as a cross-platform mobile app with Firebase real-time sync
Delivered the Group of Seven Trail App with Bluetooth proximity triggers and C++ native integrations
Established a consistent design-from-the-user-down workflow across every project

title: Owning the Stack
summary: Became the principal engineer responsible for all web platforms, tools, and production infrastructure. Added DevOps ownership to the engineering role — deployment pipelines, stability, and system reliability became part of the mandate.
highlights: Promoted to Principal Software and DevOps Engineer
Took ownership of deployment pipelines and production infrastructure stability
Designed and shipped NextShift, a real-time community feedback and voting platform
Codified a consistent engineering approach: design it like a user, build it like an engineer, stabilize it like a DevOps lead

title: UX-First Engineering
summary: The philosophy is fully operational: every flow, button, color, API, and deployment decision starts with the same question — as a user, what would I expect right here? Engineering and UX are not separate concerns.
highlights: Full ownership across frontend, backend, mobile, and infrastructure at How To Hockey
Every system decision anchored to a single mental model: what would the user expect here?
Builds tools that simplify complexity rather than expose it
Values reliability as part of user experience, not separate from it
